name: Camiño de Fisterra: Corcubión to Cape Finisterre Loop
name_original: Cee - vuelta por Faro de Finistere
description: Road cycling the Cee - tour around Finistere Lighthouse offers a moderate 22.4-mile route with 2149 feet of gain along the dramatic coast.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cee are characterized by a coastal landscape with varied terrain, including scenic roads along the Atlantic and climbs to viewpoints. The region features a mix of coastal paths, beaches, and elevated sections offering panoramic views. Road cyclists can expect routes that traverse both flat stretches and areas with significant elevation gain, such as those leading to waterfalls or capes. Best road cycling routes around Cee The most popular road cycling route is Camiño de Fisterra: Corcubión to Cape Finisterre Loop, a 22.4 miles (36.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with significant elevation gain as it circles Cape Finisterre. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Cee – Coastal Road to Muros loop from Cee, a moderate 49.5 miles (79.7 km) path. This route follows the coastal road, providing extensive views of the coastline and the sea. Local road cyclists also love the View of Carnota Beach – Coastal Road to Muros loop from Gures, a 66.2 miles (106.5 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past Carnota Beach, often completed in about 5 hours 22 minutes. Road cycling around Cee is defined by coastal roads, scenic viewpoints, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal loops to more difficult climbs. The routes in Cee are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cee's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain defines road cycling routes around Cee?
A: Road cycling routes around Cee are characterized by a diverse coastal landscape. You can expect scenic roads along the Atlantic, as well as climbs to various viewpoints. The region offers a mix of flat stretches and areas with significant elevation gain, often leading to natural features like waterfalls or capes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Cee area?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes available around Cee, offering a range of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Cee?
A: The routes in Cee cater to various abilities. There are 8 moderate routes and 7 difficult routes, providing options for both experienced cyclists and those looking for a challenge.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant climbs or elevation gain?
A: Yes, many routes feature significant elevation gain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968730172/camino-de-fisterra-corcubion-to-cape-finisterre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camiño de Fisterra: Corcubión to Cape Finisterre Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes substantial climbing as it circles Cape Finisterre. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38238741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Viewpoint Climb – Xallas Waterfall loop from Gures&lt;/a&gt;, which features a notable climb to the Ézaro Viewpoint.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Cee?
A: You can encounter several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38238741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Viewpoint Climb – Xallas Waterfall loop from Gures&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4803815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might offer views of beaches like Carnota Beach, or lead to capes such as Cape Finisterre.

Q: Are there any lighthouses or significant landmarks to visit while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talón Beach – Cape Finisterre Lighthouse loop from Cee&lt;/a&gt; will take you to the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/218305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fisterra Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass other notable points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3859881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moa Summit (Monte Pindo)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4588356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim's Boot Sculpture&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cee?
A: The road cycling routes in Cee are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, scenic coastal views, and the challenge offered by the climbs.

Q: Are there any long-distance coastal routes suitable for road biking?
A: Yes, for a longer coastal experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24610029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cee – Coastal Road to Muros loop from Cee&lt;/a&gt;. This route follows the coastline for nearly 50 miles, providing extensive views of the sea and surrounding areas.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by specific beaches?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40355122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Carnota Beach – Coastal Road to Muros loop from Gures&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, leading through coastal areas and directly past the beautiful Carnota Beach.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Cee?
A: Many of the routes around Cee are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968730172/camino-de-fisterra-corcubion-to-cape-finisterre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camiño de Fisterra: Corcubión to Cape Finisterre Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talón Beach – Cape Finisterre Lighthouse loop from Cee&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for a road cycling route in Cee?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. Shorter, moderate loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968730172/camino-de-fisterra-corcubion-to-cape-finisterre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camiño de Fisterra: Corcubión to Cape Finisterre Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take under 2 hours, while longer, more difficult routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40355122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Carnota Beach – Coastal Road to Muros loop from Gures&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours.

Drive / go, in any case a little more up the mountain, you will be rewarded by a breathtaking sight.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Cee - tour around Finistere Lighthouse?
faqs.answer: This road cycling tour is approximately 36.1 km long and generally takes around 1 hour and 57 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate for continuous cycling and does not include stops for sightseeing or refreshments.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Finistère is characterized by continuous climbs and descents, making for a hilly ride. While mostly on well-paved surfaces, you should expect gradients exceeding 10% or even 15% in places. These steep ascents can follow one another, leading to a considerable cumulative elevation gain of about 655 meters. Some sections may offer flatter stretches for variation.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect breathtaking views of the dramatic and wild Finistère coastline. The route offers rugged cliffs, picturesque fishing villages, idyllic beaches, and extensive dune systems. The constant presence of the Atlantic Ocean and the interplay of land and sea create a constantly changing and spectacular backdrop.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Cee - tour around Finistere Lighthouse?
faqs.answer: Key highlights on this route include the View of Cee, offering panoramic vistas of the town, and the iconic Cape Finisterre Lighthouse, a historic structure marking the 'End of the World'. The entire Finistère region is known for its high concentration of lighthouses and rich maritime history.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this route requires a good fitness level due to its continuous climbs and descents, with some steep gradients. While it's mostly on well-paved surfaces, beginners might find the sustained elevation changes challenging. It is more suitable for experienced cyclists or those with good endurance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in Finistère is generally during the spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) when the weather is milder and more stable, and the tourist crowds are smaller. Summers can be pleasant but may see more visitors. Always check the local weather forecast, as coastal conditions can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this tour in Cee?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the exact starting point in Cee are not provided, Cee is a town with various public parking options. Look for designated parking areas within the town, especially near the waterfront or town center, which would offer convenient access to the route.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Significant portions of the tour follow sections of the Camiño de Fisterra-Muxía (58%) and the Mtb Fisterra-Muxía (56%). Smaller segments also touch upon O Camiño dos Faros: Etapa 8 Nemiña-Faro Fisterra, Furna Grande, and A Moiña, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Cee - tour around Finistere Lighthouse dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While road cycling routes themselves don't typically have restrictions on dogs, bringing a dog on a racebike tour of this difficulty and length might be challenging. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can comfortably keep pace or be safely transported, and always adhere to local leash laws, especially in populated areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle the Cee - tour around Finistere Lighthouse. The route primarily uses public roads and paths. However, individual attractions or lighthouses along the way, such as the Cape Finisterre Lighthouse, may have their own entry fees if you wish to go inside.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through or near picturesque fishing villages and towns like Cee and Fisterra, you will find opportunities for refreshments. Look for local cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can enjoy fresh seafood and Breton specialties. It's always a good idea to carry some snacks and water, especially on longer stretches between towns.
